sourceNote: Curated from free public tools; inspired by @CoderDaMing's list — debate-relevant subset only.

tools.name: Semantic Scholar
tools.description: Free AI-powered academic search — papers, citations, and summaries.

tools.name: Google Scholar
tools.description: Search peer-reviewed articles, theses, and court opinions.

tools.name: Our World in Data
tools.description: Open charts on poverty, inequality, and economic history — cite in debates.

tools.name: FRED (St. Louis Fed)
tools.description: Federal Reserve economic data — wages, inflation, GDP, employment.

tools.name: Fraser Economic Freedom of the World
tools.description: Cross-country economic freedom data used in Nordic and growth claims.

tools.name: Heritage Index of Economic Freedom
tools.description: Annual economic freedom rankings and component scores.

tools.name: World Bank Poverty & Inequality Platform
tools.description: Official extreme poverty and inequality series for global living-standards claims.

tools.name: Project Gutenberg
tools.description: 70,000+ free public-domain ebooks — classics beyond our bundled library.

tools.name: Open Library
tools.description: Search millions of books — borrow or read public-domain scans.

tools.name: Internet Archive Texts
tools.description: Millions of free digitized books and primary sources.

tools.name: OpenStax
tools.description: Free peer-reviewed college textbooks — economics, government, history.

tools.name: Online Library of Liberty
tools.description: Scholarly public-domain editions — Tocqueville, Bastiat, Federalist, classical liberals.

tools.name: Marxists Internet Archive
tools.description: Primary socialist texts for steelman reading — Marx, Engels, Lenin, and more.

tools.name: Wayback Machine
tools.description: Access archived versions of any webpage — catch moved or deleted sources.

tools.name: Save Page Now
tools.description: Permanently snapshot a URL before it disappears.

tools.name: MIT OpenCourseWare
tools.description: Top university lectures — economics, political science, history.

tools.name: Mises Institute Library
tools.description: Free economics and liberty texts online.

tools.name: Excalidraw
tools.description: Infinite whiteboard — map argument structure and fallacies visually.

tools.name: Zotero
tools.description: Free citation manager — save sources while researching claims.
